MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-26-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Justin Hsiang, Esq. 415-741-1123 for Defendant SEAN CHANDLER.
No Other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)
The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SEAN CHANDLER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant Sean M. Chandler's motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's second amended complaint consists of 221 pages of largely unintelligible allegations and evidentiary material purportedly giving rise to 18 different causes of action against Mr. Chandler, who owns a unit in a four-unit building in which plaintiff Gurson S. Dang (who is representing himself in pro per) owns a second unit. Despite prior orders granting plaintiff leave to amend, the complaint still fails to allege facts sufficient to state any cause of action and is uncertain (ambiguous and unintelligible). The motion is granted on that ground alone. (See Stolz v. Wong Communications Limited Partnership (1994) 25 Cal.App.4th 1811, 1817 [the moving party "'need not . . . respond to assertions which are unintelligible or make out no recognizable legal claim,'" and court may treat a motion for summary judgment as a motion for judgment on the pleadings] [affirming summary judgment where complaint failed to state a cause of action.)

Further, plaintiff does not contend in his opposition to the motion that there are any disputed issues of material fact, nor does he present any argument regarding the facts or the law. Rather, he opposes the motion on the sole ground that defendants did not submit a certified copy of his deposition transcript, but instead submitted an uncertified rough draft of that transcript. Plaintiff's 59-page declaration (with exhibits) in opposition to the motion relates almost entirely to that issue. However, Plaintiff's contention is false: the deposition transcript included in defendant's appendix of evidence as Exhibit II is a certified copy of that transcript dated June 13, 2019. Finally, plaintiff's separate statement does not comply with Code Civ. Proc. 437c(b)(3) or Cal. R. Ct. 3.1350(f) in that it fails to refer to evidence supporting his position that facts are disputed.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
